spatialElements.whyThisRepresentation: The user is acting on a real / simulated object — emphasize it in place with a bright shader so the serviced part is unmistakable, rather than describing it in text.

spatialElements.title: Four strokes, in order

sourceContent.title: Four strokes, in order
items: Intake — piston down, intake valve open, exhaust valve shut. Air-fuel mix fills the cylinder.
Compression — both valves shut. Piston rises, squeezing the charge.
Power — spark plug fires at top of stroke. Combustion drives the piston down — this is where torque is produced.
Exhaust — exhaust valve opens, piston rises and pushes burnt gas out.

interactions.behavior: expand this panel to a fuller view

spatialElements.whyThisRepresentation: A list is read top-to-bottom — a vertical 2D panel beats any 3D arrangement.
spatialElements.whyThisPlacement: Persistent reference content (status, insurance, history) belongs to the user's left so it stays glanceable without competing with the repair target straight ahead. — Resolved to wall.north (wall, 6.0m × 2.7m, area 16.2m²).

surfaces.note: Largest user-facing wall — preferred mount for wall_dashboard elements.
Hero workbench — primary surface for tabletop_model + highlighted_target.

explanation.written: The prompt 'How does a four-stroke engine work?' is asking for an explanation of a mechanism's working principle. A good answer reveals what's inside the engine block (you can't explain a four-stroke without showing the piston, valves and crank), shows the four strokes in motion (Intake → Compression → Power → Exhaust — the timing is the point), and labels the parts in place so the user can tie what they see to the names they'll hear elsewhere.

mechanics.why: Reveal the interior so the moving parts are visible — a four-stroke is unintelligible from the outside.
Cut the block along the cylinder axis so the user can see the cylinder, piston, valves, and head at once.
Loop the four strokes so the user can see the timing relationships between piston, valves, and crank.

presentation.note: Default flat stage 1.5m ahead of the user. Room-aware composition is a wrapper for later.

reasoningSummary: Prompt "How does a four-stroke engine work?" was understood as domain "mechanical_explanation" with 8 content items. Spatial mix: 1× highlighted_target, 6× anchored_callout, 1× two_d_panel. Textual and numeric content stayed as readable panels rather than being forced into 3D widgets.
